notes: Encounters fire on RIDE at scales 1-3, drawn deterministically from the address hash per terrain. effects: grit/silver/lore deltas. choices cost and pay. The territory is not generated. It is woven.

dust.text: a preacher walks the dust with no water and no complaint. 'the territory,' he says, in the tone of a man finishing an argument you didn't hear the start of.

choices.label: ride on
choices.text: you leave him to his sermon. the dust takes his voice by the third hex.

choices.label: share water
choices.text: he drinks like a man who forgot he could. 'she watches the middle,' he says, pointing nowhere. that is lore, if you can hold it.

dust.text: cattle bones, laid out neat. they point northeast. cattle bones do not point.

choices.label: study them
choices.text: the arrangement repeats · seven bones, one center, six around. you have seen this shape before. you are standing on it.

choices.label: scatter them
choices.text: you kick them apart. it feels good for one hex and wrong for six.

bone.text: a peddler with a cart of salt licks and one silver mirror, face-down. 'the mirror's not for sale,' she says, before you ask. you were going to ask.

choices.label: buy salt
choices.text: salt keeps. so does the way she counted your silver without touching it.

choices.label: ask about the mirror
choices.text: 'it looks east,' she says. 'i keep it face-down so it can't.' she will say nothing else, and the nothing costs you nothing, and teaches plenty.

bone.text: a town sign, bleached blank. under the white, if you angle your hat's shadow: seven letters, and the seventh is an X.

choices.label: note it and ride
choices.text: some names want to be forgotten. you oblige, and remember anyway.

scrub.text: a coyote paces you for three hexes at exactly the distance a rifle argues about. it is not hunting. it is reading.

choices.label: tip your hat
choices.text: the coyote stops. considers. does something with its ears that in a man you'd call laughter. it peels off south.

choices.label: warning shot
choices.text: the shot flattens across the scrub. the coyote does not flinch, which tells you what it is, which is worth the round.

scrub.text: a cold campfire, set in a perfect ring, stones from seven different hexes. somebody built a small territory and burned something in the middle of it.

choices.label: read the ashes
choices.text: hair, paper, a bootlace. an unweaving, done crude and local. whoever did it learned just enough to be dangerous to themselves.

choices.label: kick it apart and camp
choices.text: you sleep in the ring. you dream in six directions at once and wake rested and unsettled in equal measure.

mesa.text: stairs cut into the black mesa, older than cutting. they go up nine steps and stop at nothing.

choices.label: climb them
choices.text: from the ninth step the territory shows its weave · hexes in hexes, out to the shimmer. you climb down slow, the way you'd back away from an animal.

choices.label: ride around
choices.text: some stairs are questions. you decline to be an answer.

salt.text: the pan is written on. wind-script, miles of it, legible only at a canter. you catch one phrase: SHE IS NOT YOUR SISTER EITHER.

choices.label: keep reading
choices.text: the script degrades into repetition · the same seven marks, tiling to the horizon. you stop before you learn to read them in your sleep.

choices.label: look at your boots
choices.text: a man who reads everything the territory writes will be writing for it inside a year. you ride on your own two eyes.

gallows.text: the gallows wood. every tree grows one bare loop of branch, low, at exactly the height. no rope in the territory was ever needed here.

choices.label: ride through slow
choices.text: you keep your hat on and your eyes forward. the wood lets you carry that dignity out the far side. it keeps a copy.

choices.label: ride around
choices.text: half a day lost. money well spent.

township.text: the township knows your name one hex before you give it. the barkeep pours what you drink. the room does not stare, which is how you know it wants to.

choices.label: drink and listen
choices.text: talk of the sisters, in the way weather is talked about · the north one's a hard winter, the east one's a red dawn. compass talk. lore, if you sort it.

choices.label: buy silver
choices.text: the smith stamps rounds while you wait and asks nothing, which costs extra.

township.text: a wanted poster with no face and no name · just a reward and an eight-pointed star where the likeness goes. the reward is high. nobody in town will meet your eye about it.

choices.label: study the star
choices.text: eight points. four you can name by now. four the poster does not label and the town does not see · you watched a woman's eyes slide off it like water off wax.
